learningCards.subject: Science
learningCards.parentSummary: This week, your child is learning about why dinosaurs went extinct in science class. Understanding this helps them learn about Earth's history and how life changes over time. Tonight, you can talk with your child about what they know about dinosaurs and ask why they think dinosaurs disappeared. This helps your child review and share their ideas.

translatedSummaries.zh: 这周您的孩子在科学课上学习恐龙灭绝的原因。了解这些原因可以帮助孩子更好地理解地球的历史和生命变化。今晚，您可以和孩子一起谈谈他们知道的恐龙，问问他们为什么恐龙会消失。这样可以帮助孩子复习并表达自己的想法。
translatedSummaries.en: This week, your child is learning about why dinosaurs went extinct in science class. Understanding this helps them learn about Earth's history and how life changes over time. Tonight, you can talk with your child about what they know about dinosaurs and ask why they think dinosaurs disappeared. This helps your child review and share their ideas.
translatedSummaries.fr: Cette semaine, votre enfant apprend en sciences pourquoi les dinosaures ont disparu. Comprendre cela l’aide à mieux connaître l’histoire de la Terre et les changements de la vie. Ce soir, vous pouvez discuter avec votre enfant de ce qu’il sait sur les dinosaures et lui demander pourquoi il pense qu’ils ont disparu. Cela l’aide à revoir ses connaissances et à exprimer ses idées.

childKnowledge.heroImageAlt: Video cover for Dinosaur Extinction (Science - G7)
childKnowledge.content:
Discover how dinosaurs disappeared from Earth millions of years ago with these fun and easy-to-understand videos. Perfect for Grade 7 students and parents to explore science together!

### 1. Getting started
Resource: Intro videos for Dinosaur Extinction
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=14QsFTEhU0M
Why: opens a beginner-friendly video list so you can choose a clear first explanation.

### 2. Build understanding
Resource: Animated worked examples for Dinosaur Extinction
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=dktnOPfE7Dc
Why: example-based explanations help connect ideas to step-by-step solving.

### 3. Practice and teach-back
Resource: Practice videos/questions for Dinosaur Extinction
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=ffHgLHTeeq0
Why: use one practice item, then explain your answer back to a parent.

learningCards.subject: Math
learningCards.teacherNotes: Focusing on mental strategies and regrouping techniques as a foundation for upcoming algebraic expressions.
learningCards.parentSummary: This week, students revisited 3-digit addition and subtraction to strengthen their mental math and understanding of place value before moving into more complex topics.

translatedSummaries.en: This week, students revisited 3-digit addition and subtraction to strengthen their mental math and understanding of place value before moving into more complex topics.
translatedSummaries.zh: 本周，学生们复习了三位数的加减法，旨在进入更复杂的课程内容前，强化他们的心算能力和对位值的理解。
translatedSummaries.fr: Cette semaine, les élèves ont revu l'addition et la soustraction à trois chiffres afin de renforcer leur calcul mental et leur compréhension de la valeur de position avant d'aborder des sujets plus complexes.

childKnowledge.heroImageAlt: Colorful math numbers and symbols for kids — 3-Digit Operations (Math · G8)
childKnowledge.content:
Here are some hand-picked videos to help you master 3-digit addition and subtraction. Watch them to sharpen your mental math skills for this week's lesson!

1. Foundational Concept: Addition with Regrouping
Video: 3-Digit Addition with Regrouping
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=mAvuom42NyY
Description: A clear walkthrough by Khan Academy on how to handle carrying over numbers when adding three-digit figures.

2. Mastering the Challenge: Subtraction with Borrowing
Video: 3-Digit Subtraction with Regrouping (Borrowing)
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=uK7v8LpX06Q
Description: Learn the step-by-step process of regrouping when the top digit is smaller than the bottom digit in subtraction.

3. Speed & Strategy: Mental Math Techniques
Video: Mental Math Addition and Subtraction Strategies
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q6_f7F-Q_Q8
Description: Discover clever shortcuts to solve 3-digit problems in your head without using a pen and paper.

tonightActions.text: Complete the 5-minute mental math challenge on your student portal.
Ask your child to solve two 3-digit subtraction problems using the 'regrouping' method.
Explain to your parents the difference between 'carrying' in addition and 'borrowing' in subtraction.

learningCards.subject: Science
learningCards.parentSummary: This week, your child is learning about acids and bases in science. This means they are discovering how different substances can be sour or slippery and how this affects everyday things like food and cleaning. You can help by asking your child to name some sour or slippery things at home and talk about where they see acids or bases in daily life. Keep it simple and fun, and praise their ideas to build confidence.

translatedSummaries.zh: 这周，您的孩子正在科学课上学习酸和碱。也就是说，他们在了解不同物质为什么有酸味或滑滑的感觉，这些知识和日常生活中的食物和清洁用品有关。您可以和孩子一起说说家里有哪些酸味或滑滑的东西，聊聊他们在哪里见过酸或碱。保持简单有趣，多鼓励孩子，让他们更有信心。
translatedSummaries.en: This week, your child is learning about acids and bases in science. This means they are discovering how different substances can be sour or slippery and how this affects everyday things like food and cleaning. You can help by asking your child to name some sour or slippery things at home and talk about where they see acids or bases in daily life. Keep it simple and fun, and praise their ideas to build confidence.
translatedSummaries.fr: Cette semaine, votre enfant apprend les acides et les bases en science. Cela signifie qu'il découvre pourquoi certaines choses sont acides ou glissantes et comment cela touche la nourriture et le nettoyage au quotidien. Vous pouvez aider en demandant à votre enfant de nommer des choses acides ou glissantes à la maison et en parlant de où ils voient des acides ou des bases dans la vie de tous les jours. Faites simple et encouragez-le pour qu'il ait confiance.

childKnowledge.heroImageAlt: Video cover for Acid vs Base (Science - G7)
childKnowledge.content:
Understanding acids and bases helps us see how everyday substances work, from the food we eat to cleaning products. This week, we'll dive into the world of acids and bases, learning how they behave, how to test them safely, and why they matter in science and our daily lives.

### 1. Getting started
Resource: Intro videos for Acid vs Base
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=1rj1oNl7hgU
Why: opens a beginner-friendly video list so you can choose a clear first explanation.

### 2. Build understanding
Resource: Animated worked examples for Acid vs Base
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=EhUpsq2i6k0
Why: example-based explanations help connect ideas to step-by-step solving.

### 3. Practice and teach-back
Resource: Practice videos/questions for Acid vs Base
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=K_nTcQknwpw
Why: use one practice item, then explain your answer back to a parent.

learningCards.classLessonTitle: Week 1 — The Magic of Color: The Color Wheel
learningCards.subject: Art
learningCards.teacherNotes: Introductory lesson on the color wheel. Students are learning to identify primary, secondary, and complementary colors to create contrast in their own artworks.
learningCards.parentSummary: This week, students explored the basics of color theory. We learned how to organize colors on a wheel and discovered how 'opposite' colors can make an artwork pop!

translatedSummaries.en: This week, students explored the basics of color theory. We learned how to organize colors on a wheel and discovered how 'opposite' colors can make an artwork pop!
translatedSummaries.zh: 本周，学生们探索了色彩理论的基础知识。我们学习了如何在色轮上组织颜色，并发现了“对比色”如何让艺术作品脱颖而出！
translatedSummaries.fr: Cette semaine, les élèves ont exploré les bases de la théorie des couleurs. Nous avons appris à organiser les couleurs sur un cercle chromatique et découvert comment les couleurs « opposées » peuvent faire ressortir une œuvre d'art !

childKnowledge.heroImageAlt: Bright and colorful illustration of art supplies and a color wheel — Color Theory (Art · G5)
childKnowledge.content:
Ready to become a color expert? Watch these 3 videos to see how artists use the color wheel to make magic happen on the canvas!

1. Intro: The Color Wheel Song
Video: The Color Wheel by Scratch Garden
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=wWW_UbrkBEw
Description: A super fun and catchy cartoon video that introduces the primary and secondary colors and how they sit on the wheel.

2. Deep Dive: How to Mix Colors
Video: Primary, Secondary and Tertiary Colors Explained
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=L1AUJ69PqM8
Description: See exactly how to mix your primary colors to create every other color in the rainbow. Perfect for your next painting session!

3. Try it: The Power of Complementary Colors
Video: Complementary Colors for Kids
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=pD9_r8X7rL4
Description: Learn about 'Color Opposites' and why putting blue next to orange makes both colors look much brighter and more exciting.

tonightActions.text: What is the complementary (opposite) color of Red on the color wheel?
Find two objects in the house that have complementary colors (like a yellow lemon and a purple toy) and put them next to each other.
Explain to your parents how you can make the color Green using only two primary colors.

learningCards.subject: Science
learningCards.parentSummary: This week, your child is learning about human teeth in science. They will review how teeth work and why they are important for eating and health. You can help by talking with your child about their teeth and asking them to name different types of teeth. This helps them remember and feel confident. Keep it simple and fun!

translatedSummaries.zh: 这周，您的孩子在科学课上学习人类的牙齿。他们会复习牙齿的作用以及牙齿对吃东西和健康的重要性。您可以和孩子聊聊他们的牙齿，让他们说出不同种类的牙齿名称。这样可以帮助他们记住知识，增强自信。保持轻松有趣！
translatedSummaries.en: This week, your child is learning about human teeth in science. They will review how teeth work and why they are important for eating and health. You can help by talking with your child about their teeth and asking them to name different types of teeth. This helps them remember and feel confident. Keep it simple and fun!
translatedSummaries.fr: Cette semaine, votre enfant apprend sur les dents humaines en sciences. Il révise comment les dents fonctionnent et pourquoi elles sont importantes pour manger et la santé. Vous pouvez aider en parlant avec votre enfant de ses dents et en lui demandant de nommer différents types de dents. Cela l’aide à se souvenir et à gagner en confiance. Faites simple et amusant !

childKnowledge.heroImageAlt: Video cover for Human Teeth (Science - G7)
childKnowledge.content:
Welcome to our exploration of human teeth! This week, we’ll discover how teeth work, why they are important, and how to take care of them. Understanding your teeth helps you keep a healthy smile and learn about the amazing biology inside your mouth.

### 1. Getting started
Resource: Intro videos for Human Teeth
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=rnIny_riefs
Why: opens a beginner-friendly video list so you can choose a clear first explanation.

### 2. Build understanding
Resource: Animated worked examples for Human Teeth
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=co4kSPqRi9o
Why: example-based explanations help connect ideas to step-by-step solving.

### 3. Practice and teach-back
Resource: Practice videos/questions for Human Teeth
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=ViKyGc-l3W0
Why: use one practice item, then explain your answer back to a parent.

learningCards.classLessonTitle: Chapter 01 — The Industrial Revolution: Global Impact
learningCards.subject: History
learningCards.teacherNotes: Replacement materials for Chapter 01. Focus on the technological shift and the environmental consequences of the era.
learningCards.parentSummary: This week, we revisited the dawn of the modern age. Students explored how the Industrial Revolution began in Britain and fundamentally changed human society through mass production and urbanization.

translatedSummaries.en: This week, we revisited the dawn of the modern age. Students explored how the Industrial Revolution began in Britain and fundamentally changed human society through mass production and urbanization.
translatedSummaries.zh: 本周，我们重新审视了现代社会的黎明。学生们探索了工业革命如何起源于英国，并如何通过大规模生产和城市化从根本上改变了人类社会。
translatedSummaries.fr: Cette semaine, nous avons revisité l'aube de l'ère moderne. Les élèves ont exploré comment la révolution industrielle a commencé en Grande-Bretagne et a fondamentalement changé la société humaine grâce à la production de masse et à l'urbanisation.

childKnowledge.heroImageAlt: Vibrant illustration of gears and digital connections — Industrial Revolution (History · G9)
childKnowledge.content:
Let's try this again! Here are 3 high-quality videos to help you visualize the steam, the gears, and the cities of the Industrial Revolution. Watch closely for the 'How' and 'Why'!

1. The Grand Overview
Video: The Industrial Revolution | National Geographic
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=xLhNP0qp38Q
Description: A breathtaking look at how a series of innovations transformed the world from muscle power to machine power.

2. The Core Technology: Coal & Steam
Video: Coal, Steam, and The Industrial Revolution: Crash Course World History #32
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=zhL5DCizj5c
Description: Join John Green to find out why the revolution happened in Britain first and how coal changed the course of history.

3. Daily Life: Working in the Factories
Video: Working Conditions in the Industrial Revolution
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=vV_vK6Bw2_8
Description: A simple, cartoon-style overview of what it was really like to work in a 19th-century factory, including the challenges faced by children.

tonightActions.text: Identify one way the invention of the steam engine changed transportation.
Find an item at home made in a factory. Imagine how someone would have made it by hand 300 years ago.
Tell your parents why 'access to coal' was a game-changer for Great Britain in the 1700s.

learningCards.subject: English
learningCards.teacherNotes: Crucial chapter for the final year. Focus is on moving from 'what' happens to 'how' it is constructed to create meaning.
learningCards.parentSummary: This week we tackled Chapter 01, focusing on 'Close Reading' and 'Visual Literacy'. The goal is for students to stop summarizing and start analyzing the author's techniques.

translatedSummaries.en: This week we tackled Chapter 01, focusing on 'Close Reading' and 'Visual Literacy'. The goal is for students to stop summarizing and start analyzing the author's techniques.
translatedSummaries.zh: 本周我们学习了第一章，重点是“细读”和“视觉素养”。目标是让学生停止简单的内容总结，开始分析作者的创作手法。
translatedSummaries.fr: Cette semaine, nous avons abordé le chapitre 01, en nous concentrant sur la « lecture attentive » et la « littératie visuelle ». L'objectif est que les élèves cessent de résumer et commencent à analyser les techniques de l'auteur.

childKnowledge.heroImageAlt: Bright study desk with books and creative supplies — Reading Strategies (Math · G12)
childKnowledge.content:
Mastering Year 12 English means reading between the lines. Here are 3 essential videos to help you move from 'Describing' to 'Analyzing' as required in Chapter 01.

1. The Foundation: How and Why We Read
Video: How and Why We Read: Crash Course Literature
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=MSYw502dJNY
Description: A high-speed guide to active reading. It explains how to engage with a text to find deeper, non-obvious meanings.

2. Viewing Strategies: Decoding Visual Texts
Video: What is Visual Literacy?
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=6m8G-C2Wk0I
Description: Essential for the 'Viewing' part of this chapter. Learn how to 'read' an image or video by analyzing composition, color, and focus.

3. Writing for Marks: Analysis vs. Description
Video: Descriptive vs. Analytical Writing
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=VlSj5-0q72M
Description: This is the 'secret sauce' for G12. It shows you exactly how to stop just retelling the story and start arguing how the text works.

tonightActions.text: Identify 3 literary techniques in your current text and explain their effect.
Look at a movie poster together. Ask your child: 'How does the lighting affect the mood?'
Explain why an 'inference' is more than just a guess based on the text.

learningCards.classLessonTitle: Week 1 — The Living World: Biological Classification
learningCards.subject: Science
learningCards.teacherNotes: Introductory lesson on taxonomy. Students should learn the Linnaean hierarchy and the concept of the Five Kingdoms.
learningCards.parentSummary: This week, students started their journey into biology by learning how scientists organize life on Earth. We explored why classification is necessary and the characteristics used to group organisms.

translatedSummaries.en: This week, students started their journey into biology by learning how scientists organize life on Earth. We explored why classification is necessary and the characteristics used to group organisms.
translatedSummaries.zh: 本周，学生们开始了生物学之旅，学习科学家如何组织地球上的生命。我们探讨了分类的必要性以及用于对生物进行分组的特征。
translatedSummaries.fr: Cette semaine, les élèves ont commencé leur voyage en biologie en apprenant comment les scientifiques organisent la vie sur Terre. Nous avons exploré pourquoi la classification est nécessaire et les caractéristiques utilisées pour grouper les organismes.

childKnowledge.heroImageAlt: Bright cartoon-style illustration of various plants and animals — Classification (Science · G7)
childKnowledge.content:
Welcome to the world of Taxonomy! Here are 3 videos to help you understand how we sort everything from tiny bacteria to giant blue whales.

1. The Big Picture: Why Classify?
Video: Classification of Living Things | Dr. Binocs Show
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=vqxomJIBGcY
Description: Join Dr. Binocs to discover why scientists need a 'filing system' for all living creatures on our planet.

2. Exploring the Groups: The Five Kingdoms
Video: The 5 Kingdoms of Classification
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=u90WvBZuV8Q
Description: A clear breakdown of the five major kingdoms of life: Animals, Plants, Fungi, Protists, and Monerans.

3. Memory Trick: The Levels of Life
Video: Taxonomy: Life's Filing System | Crash Course Kids
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=DVouAdj44mo
Description: Learn a fun mnemonic to remember the hierarchy of life, from Kingdom all the way down to Species!

tonightActions.text: Can you name the 5 Kingdoms of life we discussed in class?
Pick five items in your kitchen and try to 'classify' them into two groups based on one shared characteristic.
Explain to your parents what 'King Philip Came Over For Great Soup' stands for in biology!

reports.title: Math 9 — Unit test: Quadratic functions (class report)
reports.summary: Mean score sits in the mid band; students who did well handled vertex form and the discriminant. The most common slip was sign errors when moving terms across the equals sign—worth one short review before the next quiz.
reports.body:
Hello families,

Here’s a concise read-out from this week’s quadratic functions unit test (paper + structured working).

What went well
• Graphing parabolas from vertex form and reading turning points.
• Most students showed clear working for factorisation when a = 1.

Focus for revision
• Double-check signs when you add/subtract both sides (small error → wrong roots).
• “Completing the square” — review Example 3 in the learning card pack.

Next step
• Optional drop-in Thursday 3:30–4:00 PM for targeted questions.

— Ms. Lee

broadcasts.title: Happy Easter Holiday
broadcasts.body:
Please note that our class will be on break for the Easter holiday. There will be no classes during this period.

We hope you and your family have a wonderful and relaxing holiday. Classes will resume after the break as usual.

Best regards,
Mr. Li

questions.question: Based on the quiz discussion, what is the main idea of “I got your message. Tell me a bit more so I can help step by step.”?
Which step should come first when reviewing?
